purpose: Convert grouped SpecificTopic candidates into final canonical SpecificTopic[] using raw-deep-dive ontology, companion-pair rules, high-risk suppression gates, and source-grounded centrality adjudication.

allowed_values: Carbon Trading and Emissions Trading Schemes
Direct Impact Drivers of Biodiversity Loss
Impacts on the State of Species
Impacts on the Extent and Conditions of Ecosystems
Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services
Measures Against Violence and Harassment in the Workplace
Relationships with Suppliers, Including Payment Practices
Information-Related Impacts on Consumers and End-Users
Personal Safety of Non-Consumers and End-Users
Social Inclusion of Consumers and End-Users
Communities' Economic, Social, and Cultural Rights

value_definitions.definition: Controls, objectives, monitoring, or improvement measures for air pollution and the quality of outdoor air.
value_definitions.tag_when: The instrument centrally regulates emissions, pollutants, monitoring, plans, or duties intended to protect or improve air quality.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only mentions air as one environmental medium in a broad law, or the central rule is climate-only greenhouse gas accounting without air pollutant controls.

value_definitions.definition: Legally defined outdoor air-quality limit values, target values, assessment methods, zones, or exceedance management for ambient air.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source sets or implements pollutant concentration limits, monitoring regimes, environmental quality standards, or compliance duties for ambient/outdoor air.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source regulates emissions at a facility or product level without setting ambient concentration objectives.

value_definitions.definition: Rules for air quality inside buildings, workplaces, homes, schools, or other enclosed spaces.
value_definitions.tag_when: The regulated object is indoor air contaminants, ventilation, indoor exposure, indoor pollutant limits, or building indoor-air performance.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only addresses outdoor air emissions or occupational exposure to a specific hazardous substance without indoor-air quality standards.

value_definitions.definition: Prevention, control, liability, or remediation of pollution of seas, oceans, coastal waters, or marine waters.
value_definitions.tag_when: The instrument targets oil spills, ship-source pollution, dumping, hazardous discharges, marine contamination, or compensation for marine pollution damage.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The law is about marine biodiversity or fisheries management without pollutant contamination as a central concern.

value_definitions.definition: Pollutant emissions from industrial installations, combustion plants, manufacturing sites, or other regulated facilities.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source sets permits, thresholds, BAT-style controls, emission limit values, monitoring, or reduction duties for industrial facilities.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The instrument regulates product composition, consumer product safety, or broad environmental quality without a facility-emissions duty.

value_definitions.definition: Management of rainwater runoff, drainage, urban runoff, or stormwater discharges.
value_definitions.tag_when: The law directly regulates stormwater systems, runoff capture, drainage permits, sewer overflow, or pollution from stormwater.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only refers to water management generally, flooding, or wastewater treatment without stormwater-specific duties.

value_definitions.definition: Identification, clean-up, liability, remediation, or management of contaminated land or polluted sites.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source requires investigation, remediation, clean-up standards, liability, or risk management for contaminated soil or land.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text protects soil generally from erosion or degradation but does not address contamination or remediation.

value_definitions.definition: Pollution caused by agricultural activity, including nutrients, manure, fertilizers, pesticides, or farm runoff.
value_definitions.tag_when: The regulated source is farming or livestock activity causing water, soil, or air pollution.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Agriculture is only incidentally affected by a general biodiversity, land-use, or water-resource measure.

value_definitions.definition: Protection of soil as a natural resource from erosion, degradation, sealing, compaction, or loss of productive capacity.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally addresses soil protection, conservation, land degradation, erosion control, or sustainable soil management.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The main concern is contaminated land clean-up or pollutant thresholds rather than soil conservation.

value_definitions.definition: Standards or threshold values for soil quality, contamination, pollutants, or soil condition.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source sets soil-quality objectives, contaminant limits, assessment methods, or quality-status duties.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source addresses general soil conservation or site remediation without defined quality thresholds or status criteria.

value_definitions.definition: Waste prevention, collection, recycling, recovery, disposal hierarchy, landfill diversion, or waste management obligations.
value_definitions.tag_when: The instrument centrally regulates waste generation, separate collection, recycling targets, recovery operations, landfill, waste shipment, or waste classification.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only mentions waste incidentally as a by-product of another regime without waste-management duties.

value_definitions.definition: Central resource-circularity transition measures: reuse, repair, durability, remanufacturing, circular design, material loops, resource productivity, or explicit circular-economy strategy.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally frames circularity, reuse/repair/durability, circular design, circular economy transition, product life extension, material loops, or resource productivity.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Do not tag solely because waste, recycling, packaging waste, WEEE, batteries, EPR, or end-of-life treatment appears. Prefer the more specific waste/EPR/product labels unless circularity itself is a central legal mechanism.

value_definitions.definition: Schemes placing financial, organizational, take-back, collection, recycling, or end-of-life responsibility on producers.
value_definitions.tag_when: The law assigns producers/importers brand owners duties for collection, recycling, reporting, fees, or take-back of products/packaging.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source regulates waste generally but does not allocate end-of-life responsibility to producers.

value_definitions.definition: Performance requirements for buildings, commonly energy, emissions, environmental, or operational performance standards.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source sets building-level performance requirements, ratings, thresholds, or compliance duties.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text concerns construction products, land-use planning, or energy equipment without building-level performance duties.

value_definitions.definition: Emission limits, environmental thresholds, or pollutant performance requirements for construction products and materials.
value_definitions.tag_when: The regulated object is a construction product/material and the source sets emission, pollutant, or environmental thresholds for that product.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The rule regulates whole-building energy performance or general industrial emissions rather than construction products.

value_definitions.definition: Collection, treatment, discharge, reuse, permitting, or performance requirements for sewage, urban wastewater, or industrial wastewater.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates wastewater systems, collecting systems, treatment plants, discharge permits, treatment standards, or effluent quality.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only concerns general water quality, water allocation, or stormwater without wastewater treatment duties.

value_definitions.definition: Legal standards, objectives, thresholds, monitoring, or classification for the quality of surface water, groundwater, drinking water, or marine/coastal waters.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source sets or implements water-quality objectives, environmental quality standards, pollutant limits, monitoring, or status classifications.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source manages water quantity, allocation, pricing, or infrastructure without quality thresholds or pollution controls.

value_definitions.definition: Planning, governance, integrated management, permits, protection, or sustainable use of water resources.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally manages water bodies, river basins, water resources, abstraction, planning, or resource status.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only sets a water-quality pollutant limit or wastewater treatment duty without broader resource management.

value_definitions.definition: Requirements to reduce water use per output, product, building, process, or service.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source mandates efficient water fixtures, water-efficiency labels, industrial water-efficiency practices, or water-use performance metrics.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The law addresses water conservation generally, water pricing, or water quality without efficiency metrics or product/process performance.

value_definitions.definition: Allocation of water rights, permits, quotas, priority uses, abstraction volumes, or scarcity-based distributions.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source governs who may withdraw/use water, how quantities are allocated, or how competing water uses are prioritized.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only addresses quality, pollution, drought response, or infrastructure without allocation rights or quantities.

value_definitions.definition: Reduction or protection of water consumption and preservation of water supplies.
value_definitions.tag_when: The law requires conservation measures, demand reduction, water-saving practices, or restrictions to preserve water resources.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only regulates pollutant levels, wastewater treatment, or water tariffs without conservation objectives.

value_definitions.definition: Preparedness, planning, emergency restrictions, risk management, or response measures for drought conditions.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly addresses drought plans, drought risk, scarcity emergencies, water restrictions, or resilience during drought.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text discusses climate adaptation or water management generally without drought-specific mechanisms.

value_definitions.definition: Management of shared rivers, lakes, groundwater, basins, or watercourses crossing national or administrative borders.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source establishes obligations for international/transboundary waters, cross-border river basins, cooperation, information exchange, or shared water bodies.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The law is domestic water management with no cross-border resource, basin, or cooperation element.

value_definitions.definition: Management of fish stocks, fishing activity, catch limits, conservation of fisheries resources, aquaculture, or marine living resources as fisheries.
value_definitions.tag_when: The regulated object is fishing, fish stock conservation, catch quotas, fishing gear, fishing vessels, or fisheries licensing.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only protects marine ecosystems or biodiversity without regulating fishing activity or fishery resources.

value_definitions.definition: Designation, management, restrictions, or conservation objectives for protected areas in marine or coastal waters.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source creates or regulates marine protected areas, protected marine zones, special conservation areas at sea, or restrictions inside those areas.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The law addresses marine pollution, fisheries, or marine strategy without protected-area designation or management.

value_definitions.definition: Planning, protection, development control, restoration, or integrated management of coastal areas and shorelines.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates coastal land/sea interfaces, shoreline development, coastal erosion, coastal ecosystem management, or coastal planning.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text is about marine pollution, fisheries, or land-use planning without coastal-zone focus.

value_definitions.definition: Infrastructure for water supply, distribution, treatment, storage, flood/drainage systems, or wastewater networks.
value_definitions.tag_when: The regulated object is physical water infrastructure, public water systems, treatment works, distribution networks, reservoirs, or related investment/standards.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The law addresses water rights or quality objectives without infrastructure requirements.

value_definitions.definition: Tariffs, charges, fees, cost recovery, taxes, or pricing incentives for water use, supply, discharge, or abstraction.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source sets or requires water tariffs, abstraction fees, wastewater charges, cost-recovery principles, or pricing incentives.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only regulates allocation, conservation, or quality without pricing or economic instruments.

value_definitions.definition: Spatial planning, zoning, environmental assessment of plans/projects, development control, or land-use decision-making.
value_definitions.tag_when: The instrument centrally regulates planning approvals, land-use plans, EIA/SEA, zoning, project consent, or spatial development impacts.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source merely affects land indirectly through environmental rules, biodiversity protection, or soil clean-up without a planning mechanism.

value_definitions.definition: Measures to reduce, avoid, price, limit, or remove greenhouse gas emissions or accelerate the low-carbon transition.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally targets greenhouse gas reductions, decarbonisation, net-zero, climate targets, carbon markets/pricing, renewable-energy expansion, energy efficiency, or mitigation planning.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Do not tag merely because a pollution, waste, transport, or environmental law has indirect climate benefits. Require an explicit climate, greenhouse gas, carbon, renewable-energy, or energy-efficiency mechanism.

value_definitions.definition: Measures to prepare for, respond to, or build resilience against physical climate impacts.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source addresses climate resilience, adaptation plans, physical climate risks, flood/heat/drought resilience, or adaptation duties.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only reduces emissions, prices carbon, or supports renewables without adaptation or resilience mechanisms.

value_definitions.definition: Legally stated renewable energy share, capacity, production, procurement, or consumption targets.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source sets numerical or programmatic renewable targets, mandates, trajectories, quota schemes, or national renewable commitments.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text simply supports renewable projects or grid access without a target or quota architecture.

value_definitions.value: Carbon Trading and Emissions Trading Schemes
value_definitions.definition: Cap-and-trade or allowance-based systems for regulated emissions, including allocation, surrender, monitoring, registries, and trading.
value_definitions.tag_when: The instrument establishes or modifies an emissions trading scheme, allowance market, cap, surrender duty, or trading registry.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only imposes a carbon tax, reporting duty, or voluntary credit program without compliance trading of allowances.

value_definitions.definition: National climate commitments, targets, or implementation duties tied to Paris Agreement nationally determined contributions.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source explicitly concerns NDC submission, implementation, updating, tracking, or achievement.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source has national climate targets but no NDC or Paris-commitment connection.

value_definitions.definition: Mandatory duties to reduce emissions by a defined amount, rate, threshold, standard, or compliance mechanism.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source imposes enforceable emissions reduction duties on sectors, products, facilities, vehicles, fuels, or states.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only monitors emissions, creates reports, or sets aspirational goals without reduction obligations.

value_definitions.definition: Rules governing capture, transport, storage, permitting, monitoring, liability, or safety of carbon dioxide storage.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly regulates geological CO2 storage, CCS projects, storage site permits, leakage, monitoring, or CCS liability.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only mentions removals, offsets, or climate mitigation generally without CCS infrastructure or CO2 storage.

value_definitions.definition: Economic instruments that place a price on greenhouse gas emissions, including taxes, fees, levies, price floors, or allowance-price mechanisms.
value_definitions.tag_when: The instrument sets a carbon tax, minimum CO2 price, emissions levy, auction price mechanism, or similar mandatory price on emissions.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source creates emissions reporting, voluntary credits, or renewable incentives without pricing emissions.

value_definitions.definition: Non-compliance carbon crediting, offsetting, certification, trading, integrity, or claims regimes.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates voluntary carbon credits, offset claims, private carbon markets, certification, registries, or integrity standards.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source concerns compliance ETS allowances, carbon taxes, or public climate targets only.

value_definitions.definition: Integration of renewable energy into electricity grids, energy markets, storage, balancing, connection, permitting, or system operation.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source addresses grid access, market rules, system balancing, storage, interconnection, dispatch, or operational integration for renewables.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only sets renewable targets or subsidies without integration into the energy system.

value_definitions.definition: Emissions trading specifically for greenhouse gases, including GHG allowances, caps, monitoring, reporting, surrender, and registries.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source clearly concerns greenhouse-gas allowance trading or an ETS focused on CO2/GHG emissions.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The trading regime is not emissions-related, or the source regulates a carbon tax or voluntary offsets rather than compliance GHG trading.

value_definitions.definition: Energy audits, energy-management systems, or efficiency improvement duties for industrial or large-enterprise operations.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source requires industrial energy audits, energy management, efficiency measures, audit reporting, or large-user efficiency programs.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source sets product energy labels or building energy performance without industrial audit duties.

value_definitions.definition: Minimum energy-performance standards, labelling, ecodesign, or efficiency requirements for products, equipment, systems, or broad energy use.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source sets minimum energy efficiency levels, labels, ecodesign rules, energy performance requirements, or efficiency obligations.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The law is about renewable energy, carbon pricing, or building construction only without energy efficiency standards.

value_definitions.definition: Energy performance, efficiency, renovation, insulation, or construction requirements for buildings.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates energy performance of buildings, construction/renovation energy standards, building certificates, or nearly zero-energy buildings.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source is product ecodesign or generic energy efficiency without a building-construction scope.

value_definitions.definition: Environmental or sustainability criteria required or encouraged in government procurement.
value_definitions.tag_when: The instrument regulates how public authorities purchase goods, works, or services using environmental, climate, or sustainability criteria.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source concerns private procurement or product standards without a public-procurement mechanism.

value_definitions.definition: Reduction of acidifying pollutants such as SO2, NOx, and ammonia that cause acid rain or ecosystem acidification.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source specifically aims to reduce acidification or acidifying emissions.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source broadly regulates air quality or industrial emissions without an acidification objective or acidifying-pollutant focus.

value_definitions.definition: Controls on air pollutants that travel across borders or obligations under long-range/transboundary air pollution regimes.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source addresses long-range air pollution, cross-border air pollutants, international emission ceilings, or transboundary cooperation.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source regulates domestic ambient air quality or facility emissions without cross-border/long-range context.

value_definitions.definition: Protection, conservation, sustainable use, monitoring, or governance of biological diversity, habitats, species, or ecosystems.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally protects biodiversity, species, habitats, ecosystems, conservation status, Natura 2000, nature protection, or biodiversity impacts.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Do not tag merely because an environmental assessment or planning rule mentions flora, fauna, or environment in passing. Require biodiversity, species, habitats, ecosystems, or nature protection as a central protected interest.

value_definitions.definition: Restoring degraded ecosystems, ecological functions, natural processes, or ecosystem condition.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source mandates restoration plans, targets, repair of degraded ecosystems, nature restoration, or ecological recovery.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only protects existing areas/species or remediates pollution without ecosystem restoration.

value_definitions.definition: Creation, designation, classification, boundaries, management, or restrictions for protected terrestrial or freshwater areas.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source designates protected areas, special conservation areas, reserves, national parks, or Natura 2000 sites.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source generally protects biodiversity or species without area designation or protected-area management.

value_definitions.definition: Restoration, maintenance, or improvement of natural habitats or habitat quality for species.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source targets habitat restoration, habitat condition, ecological corridors, breeding/resting sites, or habitat conservation duties.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source protects species generally or designates areas without habitat restoration or habitat-condition duties.

value_definitions.definition: Protection, recovery, trade restrictions, taking prohibitions, or conservation measures for threatened/endangered species.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source specifically protects endangered/threatened/protected species, wild birds, fauna/flora, or sets species recovery measures.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source concerns broad biodiversity or habitats without species-specific protection.

value_definitions.definition: Prevention, early detection, eradication, containment, trade controls, or management of invasive alien species.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly regulates invasive species lists, import/keeping bans, eradication, control plans, or pathway management.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only mentions species protection or biodiversity without invasive-species mechanisms.

value_definitions.definition: Regulation of trade, import, export, transport, possession, or commercialization of wildlife, species, specimens, or CITES-listed products.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source controls wildlife trade, permits, certificates, CITES implementation, import/export of species, or trade in specimens.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source protects wildlife habitat or biodiversity without a trade/import/export mechanism.

value_definitions.definition: Protection of bees, pollinators, pollination services, or measures reducing harms to pollinator species.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source specifically regulates pollinators, bee protection, pesticide impacts on pollinators, or pollinator habitat measures.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source addresses biodiversity, pesticides, or agriculture generally without pollinator-specific focus.

value_definitions.definition: Protection of marine ecosystems, marine environmental status, marine biodiversity, or marine resources beyond pollutant control.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source targets marine environmental status, marine ecosystem protection, marine strategies, marine living resources, or sea-area conservation.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The central issue is oil spills/ship pollution only, which should be Marine Pollution; or fishing quotas only, which should be Fisheries Management.

value_definitions.value: Direct Impact Drivers of Biodiversity Loss
value_definitions.definition: Drivers that directly cause biodiversity loss, including land-use change, exploitation, invasive species, pollution, climate change, and ecosystem disturbance.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source identifies or regulates direct biodiversity-loss drivers as a substantive topic, not merely a generic environmental effect.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The law targets a single driver such as pollution or climate without linking it to biodiversity impacts.

value_definitions.value: Impacts on the State of Species
value_definitions.definition: Impacts on species populations, distribution, conservation status, extinction risk, or abundance.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source requires assessment, prevention, disclosure, or mitigation of impacts on species state or conservation status.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only protects habitat or broad biodiversity without species-state or population impact focus.

value_definitions.value: Impacts on the Extent and Conditions of Ecosystems
value_definitions.definition: Impacts on ecosystem area, distribution, fragmentation, degradation, ecological integrity, or condition.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source requires assessment, prevention, disclosure, or mitigation of impacts on ecosystem extent or condition.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source mentions ecosystems generally without an impact/condition/extent framework.

value_definitions.value: Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services
value_definitions.definition: Impacts on, risks to, or dependencies on services that ecosystems provide to people and businesses.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly references ecosystem services, dependencies, nature-related services, or impacts on the benefits provided by ecosystems.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source protects biodiversity generally without service/dependency concepts.

value_definitions.definition: Statutory minimum wage rates, minimum remuneration floors, or wage-setting mechanisms.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source sets, implements, enforces, or adjusts minimum wage or statutory minimum remuneration.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text concerns pay equity, living wages, wage reporting, or labor contracts without minimum wage standards.

value_definitions.definition: Formation, terms, transparency, information, rights, obligations, or termination of employment contracts/relationships.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates written terms, employee information, employment relationship status, contract conditions, probation, termination, or employment terms.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source regulates broad workforce rights without contract terms or employment relationship rules.

value_definitions.definition: Equal treatment and prohibition of discrimination based on protected characteristics in employment or services.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source prohibits discrimination or requires equal treatment based on gender, race, disability, age, religion, sexual orientation, or similar traits.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only promotes diversity or equal pay without legal anti-discrimination duties.

value_definitions.definition: General health and safety protections for people, often in work or product contexts when the source does not fit a narrower safety label.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally protects health and safety but the regulated context is broader than occupational safety or consumer product safety.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Use Occupational Health and Safety for workplace hazards and Product Safety for product risks when those are the central context.

value_definitions.definition: Quality, safety, conditions, and organization of the workplace environment beyond a single specific labor right.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source addresses working environment, physical/psychosocial conditions, workplace conditions, or rest/leave as workplace conditions.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source fits a narrower label such as Working Time, Mental Health, Ergonomics, or Occupational Health and Safety.

value_definitions.definition: Workplace design and practices that reduce musculoskeletal strain or adapt work to human physical capabilities.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly regulates ergonomics, manual handling, workstation design, musculoskeletal risk, or repetitive-strain prevention.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only refers generally to occupational safety or workplace health without ergonomic hazards.

value_definitions.definition: Psychological health, stress, psychosocial risks, wellbeing, or mental-health protections connected to work or regulated services.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates mental health, psychosocial risks, stress, burnout, harassment-related wellbeing, or support duties.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source regulates general occupational safety or harassment without mental-health or psychosocial-risk framing.

value_definitions.definition: Rights of workers to join, form, or participate in trade unions or worker organizations.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source protects union membership, organizing, worker representation, or association rights.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only addresses collective bargaining outcomes or social dialogue without association rights.

value_definitions.definition: Negotiation and agreements between employers and worker representatives or trade unions over employment conditions.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates collective agreements, bargaining rights, coverage, negotiation procedures, or employer duties to bargain.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only protects union association or consultation without bargaining or collective agreements.

value_definitions.definition: Protections for individuals, especially workers, who report wrongdoing, breaches, misconduct, or legal violations.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source establishes worker/reporting-person protections against retaliation, reporting channels, confidentiality, or remedies.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Use Protection of Whistleblowers when the central framing is business-conduct/governance systems rather than worker protection.

value_definitions.definition: Workplace hazard prevention, exposure limits, protective measures, risk assessment, training, monitoring, and employer safety duties.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates workplace safety, occupational exposure, employer duties, risk assessment, work equipment, carcinogens at work, or worker protection.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source is general public health, consumer product safety, or environmental emissions without workplace exposure duties.

value_definitions.definition: Planning, training, response, evacuation, resilience, or emergency procedures for incidents affecting workers or operations.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source requires emergency plans, preparedness, response systems, drills, evacuation, or major-accident readiness.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only imposes general safety duties without emergency planning or incident-response mechanisms.

value_definitions.definition: General workforce management, employment practices, labor standards, worker treatment, or HR practices not captured by a narrower labor label.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source broadly regulates workforce practices and no more specific labor label captures the central duty.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Use narrower labels such as Employment Contracts, Working Time, Minimum Wage, Non-Discrimination, OHS, or Collective Bargaining when those are central.

value_definitions.definition: Fair treatment of workers through labor standards, decent work, equitable conditions, and protection from exploitative labor practices.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source sets broad fair labor standards or labor-rights duties that are not limited to a single narrower topic.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source is only about wages, discrimination, forced labor, child labor, or contracts; tag the specific topic instead.

value_definitions.definition: Prevention, prohibition, due diligence, remediation, or enforcement related to forced labor, modern slavery, trafficking, or servitude.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly addresses forced labor, trafficking, modern slavery, supply-chain exploitation, or due diligence for those harms.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only addresses general labor standards or child labor without forced labor or trafficking.

value_definitions.definition: Prohibition, prevention, due diligence, or restrictions concerning labor by children or young persons.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates child labor, worst forms of child labor, minimum working age, youth labor protections, or supply-chain child labor.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source is about education, child welfare, or general labor without child labor provisions.

value_definitions.definition: Stability and security of employment, including protection against precarious work, arbitrary dismissal, or insecure contracts.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly regulates job security, dismissal protections, permanent/temporary contracts, or precarious employment safeguards.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source addresses general employment contracts without security/precariousness as a central issue.

value_definitions.definition: Rules on working hours, rest periods, breaks, annual leave, night work, overtime, or maximum working time.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally regulates working-time limits, rest, annual leave, overtime, night work, or scheduling.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source mentions work-life balance or workplace conditions without time/rest/leave rules.

value_definitions.definition: Wage adequacy, living wage, fair remuneration, wage sufficiency, or income adequacy beyond statutory minimum wage mechanics.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source addresses adequate/living/fair wages, wage sufficiency, wage adequacy criteria, or wage-setting for decent living.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only sets a statutory minimum wage rate; tag Minimum Wage instead unless adequacy is central.

value_definitions.definition: Consultation, information, participation, or dialogue between workers, worker representatives, employers, and public authorities.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally regulates consultation, information, negotiation, participation, worker representatives, works councils, trade unions, or collective processes between employers and workers.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Do not tag for ordinary employment information duties or general labor protections unless worker representation, consultation, or collective participation is a central mechanism.

value_definitions.definition: Rights and policies supporting balance between work and family/private life, including parental, paternity, carers, flexible work, or leave entitlements.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates parental leave, carers' leave, flexible working, work-life balance directives, or maternity/paternity balancing rights.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only regulates annual leave or working hours without family/private-life balance objectives.

value_definitions.definition: Equal pay, gender equality, pay transparency, equal treatment between men and women, or gender-based employment equality.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source addresses equal pay, gender discrimination, gender equality, pay transparency, or equal treatment of men and women.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source addresses non-discrimination generally without a gender/pay focus.

value_definitions.definition: Worker training, skills development, upskilling, professional development, or competence-building duties.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source requires employee training, skills plans, qualifications, competence, retraining, or development opportunities.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text mentions training only as incidental compliance instruction for safety or product use; tag the central topic instead.

value_definitions.definition: Inclusion, accessibility, reasonable accommodation, equal treatment, or participation of persons with disabilities.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source specifically protects or supports persons with disabilities in employment, services, accessibility, or accommodations.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source has broad non-discrimination without disability-specific rules or inclusion measures.

value_definitions.value: Measures Against Violence and Harassment in the Workplace
value_definitions.definition: Prevention, reporting, investigation, sanctions, or remedies for workplace violence, bullying, intimidation, harassment, or sexual harassment.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly regulates workplace harassment or violence, prevention plans, reporting channels, employer duties, or remedies.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only addresses discrimination or mental health without harassment/violence mechanisms.

value_definitions.definition: Diversity in workforce, leadership, boards, hiring, representation, or inclusion across demographic groups.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source requires or encourages diversity policies, representation, quotas, diversity disclosures, or inclusive practices.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source addresses a specific equality right such as non-discrimination, disability inclusion, or gender equal pay; use the specific label.

value_definitions.definition: Access to safe, secure, affordable, habitable, and adequate housing, including worker housing when regulated.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly addresses adequate housing rights, accommodation standards, worker housing, or housing access/conditions.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source concerns buildings, land-use planning, or construction without housing adequacy or rights.

value_definitions.definition: Prevention, detection, reporting, enforcement, or governance controls for corruption, misuse of entrusted power, conflicts of interest, or improper influence.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally regulates corruption offences, anti-corruption controls, public/private corruption, conflict-of-interest rules, or corruption prevention.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only addresses general compliance, corporate governance, or financial crime without corruption.

value_definitions.definition: Prevention, prohibition, detection, reporting, or sanctions for giving, receiving, offering, or soliciting bribes.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source specifically regulates bribery, bribe offers, facilitation payments, gifts/hospitality as bribes, or bribery offences.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source addresses corruption broadly without bribery-specific mechanisms, unless bribery is expressly named.

value_definitions.definition: Reporting or disclosure of environmental, social, governance, sustainability, or responsible-business information.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally requires ESG/sustainability disclosures, ESG statements, management reports, non-financial statements, or related reporting architecture.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source includes operational reporting to regulators only, such as permits or emissions returns, without ESG or sustainability disclosure architecture.

value_definitions.definition: Processes to identify, prevent, mitigate, account for, and remedy ESG, human-rights, environmental, or supply-chain adverse impacts.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source requires due diligence over human rights, environmental impacts, supply chains, value chains, forced labor, child labor, or sustainability risks.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only imposes reporting or general compliance without a due-diligence process.

value_definitions.definition: Disclosure of sustainability impacts, risks, opportunities, policies, metrics, or targets, often under CSRD/ESRS-style reporting.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source specifically requires sustainability reports, sustainability statements, ESRS disclosures, or sustainability information in annual reports.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source merely requires environmental monitoring or financial statements without sustainability disclosure.

value_definitions.definition: Disclosure of non-financial information such as environmental, social, employee, human-rights, anti-corruption, or diversity matters.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source refers to non-financial reporting/statements or implements non-financial disclosure requirements.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source is financial reporting only or operational regulatory reporting without public non-financial disclosure.

value_definitions.definition: Central regulation of legal-compliance architecture, compliance-management systems, authorisation/register regimes as the regulated object, certification/conformity status, or economic/criminal compliance offences.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally regulates compliance systems, legal conformity programs, approval/certification status, regulated registers, broad compliance duties, or offence frameworks as the subject of the instrument.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Do not tag for ordinary legal obligations, permits, monitoring, reporting, competent authorities, enforcement provisions, transposition duties, penalties, sanctions, or the fact that the source is a law. Suppress unless compliance architecture is itself a central regulated object.

value_definitions.definition: Accounting, financial statements, annual accounts, audit, transparency, securities reporting, or financial disclosure obligations.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates financial statements, accounting rules, annual accounts, audit reports, transparency reports, or financial disclosure.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source concerns sustainability/non-financial reporting without financial statements or accounting.

value_definitions.definition: Corporate responsibility commitments, policies, or obligations concerning social/environmental impacts and responsible business conduct.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source explicitly addresses CSR, responsible business conduct, corporate responsibility policies, or broad corporate accountability.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source has a specific ESG due-diligence, reporting, labor, environmental, or governance topic; use that specific label unless CSR is central.

value_definitions.definition: Corporate political contributions, lobbying, advocacy, public-policy influence, registration, transparency, or related controls.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates lobbying, political donations, public-policy engagement, registers of lobbyists, or transparency of political influence.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source mentions government interaction only as normal regulatory compliance or permitting.

value_definitions.definition: Governance/business-conduct systems protecting whistleblowers or reporting persons from retaliation.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source establishes whistleblowing channels, confidentiality, anti-retaliation duties, governance controls, or reporting protections.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Use Whistleblower Protection when the primary framing is worker rights in own workforce rather than governance conduct.

value_definitions.value: Relationships with Suppliers, Including Payment Practices
value_definitions.definition: Supplier relationship management, purchasing practices, fair payment, late payment, procurement conduct, or supplier terms.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly regulates supplier due diligence, payment terms/practices, purchasing practices, supply-chain relationship governance, or supplier transparency.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source merely affects suppliers indirectly through product or environmental duties.

value_definitions.definition: Reporting, disclosure, handling, investigation, or consequences of actual corruption or bribery incidents.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source requires disclosure, investigation, enforcement, registers, or reporting of corruption/bribery incidents.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source establishes general anti-corruption policies but does not address incidents or incident reporting.

value_definitions.definition: Values, governance culture, ethical standards, integrity systems, codes of conduct, or compliance culture within an organization.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates corporate culture, ethics programs, integrity systems, conduct policies, gifts/hospitality culture, or board oversight of conduct.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The text only creates legal compliance duties without culture, ethics, or conduct-system framing.

value_definitions.definition: Safety requirements, risk controls, conformity, market surveillance, warnings, or liability for goods/services placed on the market.
value_definitions.tag_when: The regulated object is product safety, defects, safety standards, toys, cosmetics, food safety, machinery/product risks, or market surveillance.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source regulates product labeling, chemical registration, or consumer rights without safety-risk controls.

value_definitions.definition: Conformity assessment, certification, licensing, accreditation, or compliance marks demonstrating that products or organizations meet requirements.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally requires certificates, conformity assessment, accreditation, certification bodies, compliance marks, or similar formal verification.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source creates legal duties but no certification/conformity process; do not tag solely for general compliance.

value_definitions.definition: Mandatory labels, markings, information, warnings, claims, traceability information, or presentation requirements for products.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates product labels, packaging information, food information, energy labels, textile labels, hazard labels, or consumer information duties on products.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source regulates product safety, design, or advertising without label/marking/information requirements.

value_definitions.definition: Central rules protecting consumers in transactions, marketing, contracts, products, services, information asymmetry, financial services, or unfair trader practices.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally protects consumers/end-users through rights, remedies, unfair commercial-practice rules, misleading-advertising controls, contract safeguards, product/service information, or consumer transaction protections.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Do not tag where consumers are only indirectly affected by environmental, labor, industrial, product-stewardship, or reporting rules. For downstream ESRS mapping, distinguish market/trader conduct from direct end-user safety or information impacts.

value_definitions.definition: Requirements for product design, manufacture, composition, durability, repairability, safety-by-design, or production specifications.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly regulates how products are designed or manufactured, including ecodesign, construction/design requirements, or material composition.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source is only about labeling, recall, waste disposal, or consumer rights after sale.

value_definitions.definition: Withdrawal, recall, corrective action, notification, or market-removal procedures for unsafe or non-compliant products.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates product recall, withdrawal from market, corrective actions, safety notifications, or recall obligations.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only sets general product safety standards without recall/withdrawal mechanisms.

value_definitions.definition: Authorization, approval, registration, renewal, or market access for biocidal products and active substances.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly regulates registration/authorization of biocidal products, active substances, dossiers, approvals, or market placement.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source is broad chemical regulation or pesticide regulation without biocidal product registration.

value_definitions.definition: Protection of personal data and privacy, including GDPR-style processing principles, rights, controllers/processors, and enforcement.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source regulates personal data, data protection, privacy rights, GDPR implementation, processing, controllers, processors, or data-subject rights.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source concerns cybersecurity, financial reporting, or non-financial reporting without personal-data/privacy duties.

value_definitions.definition: Combined data privacy and non-financial/ESG disclosure obligations, especially where privacy-related information is part of sustainability or non-financial reporting.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source explicitly connects personal data/privacy with non-financial or sustainability reporting obligations.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source is only privacy/GDPR or only non-financial reporting; tag those separately instead.

value_definitions.value: Information-Related Impacts on Consumers and End-Users
value_definitions.definition: Consumer/end-user impacts from information, privacy, data, content, transparency, accessibility of information, or misleading/insufficient information.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly regulates information-related consumer/end-user impacts, privacy notices, data impacts, product information, digital information, or transparency to users.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source is generic data protection with no consumer/end-user framing, or product labeling without wider information-impact context.

value_definitions.value: Personal Safety of Non-Consumers and End-Users
value_definitions.definition: Physical safety of consumers or end-users from product, service, facility, or operational risks; keep this canonical label even though the wording says 'Non-Consumers'.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly addresses physical safety, injury risk, health harm, dangerous products/services, or safety of users/end-users.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source is about privacy, social inclusion, or general consumer rights without personal safety risk.

value_definitions.value: Social Inclusion of Consumers and End-Users
value_definitions.definition: Fair access, non-discrimination, accessibility, inclusion, affordability, or equitable treatment of consumers/end-users.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source protects access or inclusion of consumers/end-users, including vulnerable groups, accessibility, affordability, non-discrimination in services, or universal access.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source concerns worker inclusion or general consumer protection without inclusion/accessibility focus.

value_definitions.definition: Prohibition, restriction, phase-out, disposal, monitoring, or elimination of persistent organic pollutants.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly regulates POPs, Stockholm Convention substances, PCB/PCT elimination, dioxins/furans, or POP waste disposal.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source broadly regulates hazardous substances or chemicals without persistent-organic-pollutant focus.

value_definitions.definition: Protection of people and environment from chemical hazards through safe handling, assessment, exposure control, classification, or risk management.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source addresses chemical hazards, safety data, exposure/risk assessment, safe use, chemical accidents, or risk management for substances/mixtures.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Use Chemical Regulation for registration/market governance, Hazardous Substances for dangerous material restrictions, or Product Labeling for label-only duties.

value_definitions.definition: Regulatory governance of chemicals, including registration, evaluation, authorization, restriction, data sharing, notification, or market access.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source establishes chemical regulatory schemes such as REACH/CLP/PIC, chemical registers, authorization, restrictions, test methods, or data-sharing obligations.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only addresses safety outcomes or hazardous-substance exposure without regulatory scheme mechanics.

value_definitions.definition: Dangerous, toxic, hazardous, radioactive, carcinogenic, mutagenic, ozone-depleting, or otherwise harmful substances/materials.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source centrally regulates dangerous chemicals, hazardous waste, POPs, asbestos, silica, radioactive waste, ozone-depleting substances, dangerous pollutants, or hazardous substance restrictions.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: Do not tag merely because a product, facility, or environmental law has potential hazards; require hazardous substances or dangerous pollutants to be central.

value_definitions.definition: Limits, bans, or restrictions on hazardous substances in products, materials, equipment, or supply chains.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source sets substance restrictions such as RoHS, banned/limited hazardous substances in electrical/electronic equipment, or product material restrictions.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source broadly regulates hazardous substances without product/content restriction limits.

value_definitions.definition: Rules for electrical or electronic equipment, including safety, waste, hazardous substances, energy performance, or product requirements.
value_definitions.tag_when: The regulated object is electrical/electronic equipment, WEEE, RoHS goods, appliances, batteries in equipment, or electronic product standards.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source regulates chemicals, waste, or energy efficiency generally without electrical/electronic equipment as the product class.

value_definitions.value: Communities' Economic, Social, and Cultural Rights
value_definitions.definition: Rights of affected communities to livelihoods, health, education, housing, culture, land, water, or other economic/social/cultural interests.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly protects or affects community rights to land, housing, cultural heritage, health, livelihood, water, education, or community wellbeing.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source has broad environmental effects on the public but no community-rights framing or affected-community mechanism.

value_definitions.definition: Community rights related to participation, consultation, access to justice, expression, assembly, security, and political/civic freedoms.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly addresses community consultation, participation, access to information/justice, civil liberties, or political rights of affected communities.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source only imposes public notice or administrative procedure without community rights as a central issue.

value_definitions.definition: Rights, consultation, consent, land, culture, self-determination, and protections specific to Indigenous peoples.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly references Indigenous peoples, traditional lands, free prior and informed consent, cultural rights, or Indigenous community protections.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source affects rural/local communities generally but does not identify Indigenous peoples or Indigenous rights.

value_definitions.definition: Protection of animal health, humane treatment, husbandry, transport, slaughter, testing, or welfare conditions.
value_definitions.tag_when: The source directly regulates animal welfare, cruelty, livestock treatment, transport/slaughter conditions, animal testing, or welfare standards.
value_definitions.do_not_tag_when: The source protects wildlife biodiversity or endangered species without welfare/treatment of animals.

centrality_levels.primary: The label is one of the main regulated objects, protected interests, legal mechanisms, or duty structures.
centrality_levels.companion: The label is a distinct central pillar that is necessary to describe the source, but not the dominant topic.
centrality_levels.incidental: The label is only mentioned as background, an affected medium, an example, an implementation detail, or an indirect consequence.
centrality_levels.reject: The label is a nearby concept but should not be emitted.

evidence_roles.regulated_object: What the law directly regulates: e.g., wastewater, packaging, emissions allowances, employment contracts.
evidence_roles.legal_mechanism: How the law operates: e.g., EPR, carbon price, reporting duty, conformity assessment.
evidence_roles.protected_interest: The protected interest: e.g., air quality, water quality, worker health, biodiversity.
evidence_roles.direct_addressee: Who must comply: e.g., employers, producers, ships, financial institutions.
evidence_roles.affected_medium: An environmental or social medium affected by the law but not necessarily the central object.
evidence_roles.implementation_actor: Member State, ministry, competent authority, regulator, court, or agency administering the regime.
evidence_roles.incidental_reference: A passing mention, example, or downstream consequence.

centrality_model.survival_rule: Only primary and strong companion candidates survive. Incidental, implementation_actor-only, background_context, and weak affected_medium candidates are dropped.

reconciliation_algorithm: 1. Reject candidates whose label is not in controlled_enum.allowed_values.
2. Re-evaluate candidate centrality from the evidence_packet; do not blindly trust family KM centrality claims.
3. Merge duplicate labels across family KMs; keep the strongest source evidence and highest valid centrality.
4. Drop candidates with centrality=reject or centrality=incidental.
5. Drop candidates whose evidence_role is only implementation_actor, background_context, incidental_reference, or weak affected_medium.
6. Keep primary candidates first. Keep companion candidates only when they represent a distinct central legal pillar with direct title/scope/operative-provision evidence.

7. Apply label_specific_survival_rules. For listed high-risk labels, companion centrality is insufficient by default; require primary centrality plus direct regulated_object/legal_mechanism/protected_interest/direct_addressee evidence.
8. Suppress high-risk generic labels unless they satisfy their strict tag_when and survival_gate tests.
9. Prefer the most specific label over a generic parent label when both are supported by the same evidence.
10. Preserve strong specific primary candidates even when another label is more central; do not drop a source-supported primary candidate solely due to label-count pressure.
11. Final SpecificTopic[] should normally contain 2-3 labels. Use 4-5 only for multiple independent regulated obligations or protected interests.
12. Return no label rather than a weak generic label when the source evidence is insufficient.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Biodiversity Conservation
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: Biodiversity, species, habitats, protected areas, conservation status, ecosystems, or nature protection are central.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Chemical Regulation
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: The source centrally regulates chemical registration, evaluation, authorization, restriction, classification/labelling/packaging, data sharing, notification, PIC/REACH/CLP-style market governance, or chemical registers.
do_not_use_for: waste laws without chemical market governance
high_risk_generic_suppressions.survival_gate: Require direct regulatory-scheme evidence, not just a chemical-like substance mention.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Chemical Safety
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: The source centrally regulates chemical hazard/risk protection, exposure control, safe handling/use, safety data, classification, risk assessment, or chemical accident/risk-management duties.
do_not_use_for: product stewardship without chemical hazard duties
high_risk_generic_suppressions.survival_gate: Require direct chemical lifecycle, exposure, hazard, risk assessment, classification, or safe-use evidence.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Circular Economy
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: The source centrally frames circularity, reuse, repair, durability, circular design, material loops, or resource-productivity strategy.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Climate Change Mitigation
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: The source has explicit climate, greenhouse gas, carbon, renewable-energy, energy-efficiency, or decarbonisation mechanisms.
do_not_use_for: environmental assessment laws with indirect climate benefits

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Compliance and Certification
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: The source centrally requires conformity assessment, certification, CE marking, accreditation, certification bodies, audit/certification status, or formal compliance schemes.
high_risk_generic_suppressions.survival_gate: Require primary centrality and direct legal_mechanism or regulated_object evidence. Companion is reject by default.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Compliance with Laws and Regulations
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: Compliance architecture, compliance management, legal conformity program, certification/compliance status, regulated register/authorization regime, or offence/compliance framework is itself the central regulated object.
do_not_use_for: the fact that the source is a law
environmental/product/labor duties with normal enforcement language
high_risk_generic_suppressions.survival_gate: Require primary centrality and direct title/scope/operative-provision support. Companion is reject by default.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Consumer Protection Laws
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: Consumer rights, unfair practices, misleading advertising, contract protections, consumer remedies, product/service information, or transaction safeguards are central.
do_not_use_for: indirect consumer benefits from environmental or labor laws

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Corporate Culture
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: The source centrally regulates corporate culture, ethics programs, codes of conduct, integrity systems, or conduct-policy governance.
high_risk_generic_suppressions.survival_gate: Require primary centrality and direct business-conduct governance evidence.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Hazardous Substances
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: Hazardous chemicals, hazardous waste, POPs, asbestos, silica, radioactive waste, ozone-depleting substances, dangerous pollutants, or comparable hazardous materials are central regulated objects.
do_not_use_for: product safety risk without hazardous substance regulation
high_risk_generic_suppressions.survival_gate: Require primary centrality and direct substance/waste/pollutant restriction, exposure, disposal, classification, or remediation evidence.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: The source directly references ecosystem services, nature-related dependencies, or impacts/dependencies on services ecosystems provide.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Impacts on the Extent and Conditions of Ecosystems
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: The source requires assessment, prevention, mitigation, disclosure, or management of ecosystem extent, condition, degradation, fragmentation, or ecological integrity as an explicit framework.
high_risk_generic_suppressions.survival_gate: Require explicit ecosystem impact/condition/extent language and operative duties.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Impacts on the State of Species
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: The source requires assessment, prevention, mitigation, disclosure, or management of species population, distribution, conservation status, extinction risk, or abundance impacts.
do_not_use_for: habitat protection without species-state impact framework
high_risk_generic_suppressions.survival_gate: Require explicit species-state/population/conservation-status impact language and operative duties.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Information-Related Impacts on Consumers and End-Users
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: The source directly regulates end-user/consumer information, product information, privacy/data notices, digital information, warnings, instructions, accessibility of information, or misleading/insufficient information affecting users.
high_risk_generic_suppressions.survival_gate: Require primary centrality and direct consumer/end-user framing.

high_risk_generic_suppressions.label: Social Dialogue
high_risk_generic_suppressions.suppress_unless: Worker representatives, unions, consultation, works councils, collective bargaining, or information/consultation processes are central.
do_not_use_for: general employee-information duties without representative/collective mechanism

cross_family_disambiguation_rules.rule: Wastewater Treatment is primary for urban wastewater/sewage/collecting systems. Marine Pollution is not added merely because receiving waters include coastal or marine waters.

cross_family_disambiguation_rules.case: Water quality vs water resource management
cross_family_disambiguation_rules.rule: Water Quality Standards requires quality objectives/standards or pollutant status. Water Resource Management requires basin/resource planning, abstraction/allocation, integrated management, or governance of water as a resource.
Waste Reduction and Recycling, EPR, Product Labeling, WEEE, or batteries do not automatically imply Circular Economy.
Pollutant limits, industrial emissions, hazardous substances, and air-quality rules do not imply Climate Change Mitigation unless greenhouse gases/carbon/energy transition are central.
Land Use Planning is primary for environmental assessment/planning laws. Add biodiversity labels only when biodiversity/species/habitats/ecosystems are distinct central protected interests.

Employment conditions, wages, working time, OHS, and discrimination are S1-style topics. Forced labor, child labor, human trafficking, or supplier due diligence is value-chain-oriented when the duties fall on companies for suppliers/imports/value chains.

cross_family_disambiguation_rules.case: Consumer market conduct vs end-user safety
cross_family_disambiguation_rules.rule: Unfair commercial practices or misleading advertising select Consumer Protection Laws but downstream ESRS may be G1 rather than S4. Product safety, labeling, privacy, recall, and end-user information remain S4-style.

For Compliance with Laws and Regulations, Compliance and Certification, Chemical Safety, Chemical Regulation, Hazardous Substances, Information-Related Impacts, Corporate Culture, and ecosystem-impact labels, do not retain companion candidates unless direct title/scope/operative support makes them independent primary-like pillars.

When a specific candidate has primary centrality plus regulated_object/legal_mechanism/protected_interest/direct_addressee evidence, preserve it even if a broader label is also present; prefer removing the broader weak label.

SpecificTopic.value: array of canonical SpecificTopic labels, max 5
SpecificTopic.tag_reason: why selected labels are central and why near-misses were rejected
SpecificTopic.evidence_excerpt: short source-supported excerpts, one or more, supporting the selected topics

validation_rules: Emit only canonical labels from controlled_enum.allowed_values.
Do not output labels from weak, incidental, background, or implementation-only evidence.
Do not use benchmark labels, filenames, row IDs, URLs, URL slugs, or exact full-title lookup tables.
Every retained label must have source evidence and centrality justification.
Re-evaluate family centrality claims; final survival requires source evidence, not merely candidate confidence.
High-risk labels require primary centrality plus direct title/scope/operative-provision support; companion evidence is reject by default unless explicitly justified.
revision_note: v3.2 raw deep-dive patch: ontology-backed SpecificTopic finalization, companion rescue, and high-risk FP suppression.

label_specific_survival_rules.label: Compliance with Laws and Regulations
label_specific_survival_rules.suppress_unless: Compliance architecture, compliance management, legal conformity program, certification/compliance status, regulated register/authorization regime, or offence/compliance framework is itself the central regulated object.
do_not_use_for: the fact that the source is a law
environmental/product/labor duties with normal enforcement language
label_specific_survival_rules.survival_gate: Require primary centrality and direct title/scope/operative-provision support. Companion is reject by default.

label_specific_survival_rules.label: Compliance and Certification
label_specific_survival_rules.suppress_unless: The source centrally requires conformity assessment, certification, CE marking, accreditation, certification bodies, audit/certification status, or formal compliance schemes.
label_specific_survival_rules.survival_gate: Require primary centrality and direct legal_mechanism or regulated_object evidence. Companion is reject by default.

label_specific_survival_rules.label: Chemical Safety
label_specific_survival_rules.suppress_unless: The source centrally regulates chemical hazard/risk protection, exposure control, safe handling/use, safety data, classification, risk assessment, or chemical accident/risk-management duties.
do_not_use_for: product stewardship without chemical hazard duties
label_specific_survival_rules.survival_gate: Require direct chemical lifecycle, exposure, hazard, risk assessment, classification, or safe-use evidence.

label_specific_survival_rules.label: Chemical Regulation
label_specific_survival_rules.suppress_unless: The source centrally regulates chemical registration, evaluation, authorization, restriction, classification/labelling/packaging, data sharing, notification, PIC/REACH/CLP-style market governance, or chemical registers.
do_not_use_for: waste laws without chemical market governance
label_specific_survival_rules.survival_gate: Require direct regulatory-scheme evidence, not just a chemical-like substance mention.

label_specific_survival_rules.label: Hazardous Substances
label_specific_survival_rules.suppress_unless: Hazardous chemicals, hazardous waste, POPs, asbestos, silica, radioactive waste, ozone-depleting substances, dangerous pollutants, or comparable hazardous materials are central regulated objects.
do_not_use_for: product safety risk without hazardous substance regulation
label_specific_survival_rules.survival_gate: Require primary centrality and direct substance/waste/pollutant restriction, exposure, disposal, classification, or remediation evidence.

label_specific_survival_rules.label: Information-Related Impacts on Consumers and End-Users
label_specific_survival_rules.suppress_unless: The source directly regulates end-user/consumer information, product information, privacy/data notices, digital information, warnings, instructions, accessibility of information, or misleading/insufficient information affecting users.
label_specific_survival_rules.survival_gate: Require primary centrality and direct consumer/end-user framing.

label_specific_survival_rules.label: Corporate Culture
label_specific_survival_rules.suppress_unless: The source centrally regulates corporate culture, ethics programs, codes of conduct, integrity systems, or conduct-policy governance.
label_specific_survival_rules.survival_gate: Require primary centrality and direct business-conduct governance evidence.

label_specific_survival_rules.label: Impacts on the Extent and Conditions of Ecosystems
label_specific_survival_rules.suppress_unless: The source requires assessment, prevention, mitigation, disclosure, or management of ecosystem extent, condition, degradation, fragmentation, or ecological integrity as an explicit framework.
label_specific_survival_rules.survival_gate: Require explicit ecosystem impact/condition/extent language and operative duties.

label_specific_survival_rules.label: Impacts on the State of Species
label_specific_survival_rules.suppress_unless: The source requires assessment, prevention, mitigation, disclosure, or management of species population, distribution, conservation status, extinction risk, or abundance impacts.
do_not_use_for: habitat protection without species-state impact framework
label_specific_survival_rules.survival_gate: Require explicit species-state/population/conservation-status impact language and operative duties.

label_specific_survival_rules.label: Impacts on and Dependencies on Ecosystem Services
label_specific_survival_rules.suppress_unless: The source directly references ecosystem services, nature-related dependencies, or impacts/dependencies on services ecosystems provide.

max_final_topics_policy.allow_four_or_five_only_when: The source contains multiple independent regulated obligations/protected interests with direct operative-provision evidence.
max_final_topics_policy.companion_default: Companion labels do not survive unless a label-specific survival rule or direct independent legal pillar supports them.

global_reconciliation_policy_v3_2: Do not trust family centrality blindly. Re-adjudicate centrality from source title, scope, definitions, addressees, and operative provisions.
Keep source-supported benchmark-expected companion labels where the ontology says the benchmark expects both broad and narrow labels.
Reject high-risk adjacent labels unless their label-specific survival rule is satisfied.
Normally output 1-3 SpecificTopic labels. Output 4-5 only when separate central legal pillars are explicit.
Do not keep semantically adjacent labels solely because they are plausible ESG coverage.
Do not drop a proposed gold-like candidate only because another label is more central; apply allowed_companion_pairs and rescue rules first.

allowed_companion_pairs_v3_2.keep_both_when: Source regulates employment access, employment conditions, dismissal, training, occupation, employment relationship, worker information, or employment equality.
Source regulates working time, rest, night work, leave, pregnancy/maternity workplace conditions, or workplace organization as health/safety/work-environment protection.
Source is broad labor/fair-work/employment standards or worker-protection framework.
Source imposes direct GHG/climate emission-reduction obligations in a climate mechanism.

labels: Carbon Trading and Emissions Trading Schemes
allowed_companion_pairs_v3_2.keep_both_when: Source has allowance surrender, caps, registry, emissions-unit compliance, or emissions trading obligations.
Source assigns end-of-life collection/recycling/fees/take-back/reporting duties to producers.
Product-risk or market-surveillance duties protect consumers/end-users.
Consumer-facing product information, warnings, claims, labels, or misleading-label controls are central.
Wastewater collection/treatment duties are tied to effluent, receiving-water, pollution, EQS, or water-quality standards.
Marine pollution prevention/remediation is explicitly linked to marine environment/resource protection beyond pollution liability.
Source regulates financial reporting through a legal compliance/market supervision architecture.

labels: Information-Related Impacts on Consumers and End-Users
mutual_exclusion_or_separate_evidence_ru.rule: Product labeling does not automatically imply Information-Related Impacts. Keep both only when consumer/end-user information harms, information rights, privacy/information asymmetry, or end-user accessibility are central.

Chemical regulation does not automatically imply Hazardous Substances. Keep both only when hazardous properties, exposure, restrictions, classification, hazardous waste, or dangerous substances are operative.
Climate mitigation does not automatically imply direct reduction obligations. Keep both only if enforceable reductions, caps, standards, or surrender obligations exist.
Biodiversity/habitat context does not imply endangered species. Keep Endangered Species Protection only with species-specific protection.
Legal-compliance architecture and formal certification/conformity assessment are distinct. Keep both only when both are central.

high_risk_label_survival_overrides_v3_2.label: Climate Change Mitigation
survive_when_any: explicit climate/GHG/carbon/decarbonisation/net-zero/renewables transition/carbon market/carbon pricing mitigation mechanism

high_risk_label_survival_overrides_v3_2.label: Information-Related Impacts on Consumers and End-Users
Fair Labor Practices
reject_when_any: single narrow workplace technical rule with no broader labor-rights framing

high_risk_label_survival_overrides_v3_2.label: Consumer Protection Laws
survive_when_any: product safety/labeling regime intended to protect consumers
reject_when_any: consumers only indirectly affected by environmental, industrial, labor, reporting, or product-stewardship duties

reconciliation_algorithm_v3_2: 1. Normalize and deduplicate all family candidates.

2. Re-adjudicate each candidate using title/scope/definitions/addressees/operative evidence and assign benchmark_centrality: core, benchmark_expected_companion, ordinary_companion, adjacent, incidental, reject.
3. Apply high_risk_label_survival_overrides_v3_2 before generic centrality thresholds.
5. Keep benchmark_expected_companion labels when allowed_companion_pairs_v3_2 or label-specific survival rules support them.
6. Reject adjacent/incidental labels unless survival overrides explicitly rescue them.
7. Apply mutual-exclusion rules and prefer benchmark-exact regulated-object labels over broad ESG semantic labels.
8. If more than 5 labels remain, keep title/scope/operative supported labels first, then regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee.
9. Preserve all rejected candidates with reasons and the source evidence role.
